body_txt: Some parents seek to control their child through the primary advantage they have, having more money than them as well as providing residence and food. Thus they are able to manipulate them through the threat of removing it or taking away what little they have. This can be especially pronounced in a Stage Mom situation, considering their children nothing more than a cash cow they can exploit for personal gain. Either the parents take the money a child earns and keep it for themselves, or they actively deny the dependent personal gain or a voice in how it is spent. This can also exist in a separate form, by withholding support unless they obey all of the parent's wishes, no matter how cruel or unreasonable, without complaint. Abuse is about control, and this is controlling them through money. Bonus points if the parents are foster parents, and keep the kids around for the welfare check. Very much Truth in Television , and enters the media spotlight regularly when one or both parents of a rising young music or acting star exploit their child's achievements. The Jackie Coogan Act was created where studios must provide child actors a sealed bank account ensuring they will have access to that money they become an adult, named after the child actor whose mother and stepfather blew through most of his money — around three to four million dollars , back in the 1920s and '30s. note That is roughly equivalent to $30-40 million in today's money after inflation. This form of abuse also exists between spouses/partners or other situations where someone is largely dependent on support from another, either when one member of a couple exploits the other financially (stealing their savings, taking out unauthorized credit lines or loans in their name, and making unauthorized and often unwise or fraudulent investments are some common methods of doing this), when they guide them towards decisions that will ensure financial dependence and limit their ability to make a living, or when they deprive the other of access to money, often as a way to keep an abused spouse from escaping. It's also frighteningly common for adult children to financially abuse elderly relatives , especially those with cognitive disabilities. Finally, another form of it is combined with religious abuse: a religion or sect or guru demands monetary donations or time that could be spent elsewhere to the extent that the donations or lifestyle damages the givers' financial well-being, and/or demands exorbitant donations for advancing in the religious system, faith healing, initiations, or similar. The Church of Happyology is infamous for this, but there have been many other less well-known examples too. Does not generally apply in situations where someone is temporarily deprived of money as a punishment or forced to pay for an at-fault incident. An example of this might be a child who caused damage being told the cost of repairing or replacing whatever they damaged will be deducted from their allowance/pocket money. This can also be downplayed where a parent refuses to provide financial support because a child, possibly even an adult child, makes moral decisions they don't agree with. The child is then forced to figure things out on their own , making it a form of Tough Love and the motivations of the parent are left more ambiguous . A Sub-Trope of Abusive Parents and Domestic Abuse ; see also Elder Abuse . A sister trope would be Fostering for Profit and Taking the Kids . Overlaps with Hoarding the Profits if the abuser tries to keep money that they and the abused made together or Pushing the Debt if the abuser forces the abused to pay off their debts or borrows money under the abused's name. noreallife
